pat-rhombus.title: Bonus: Rhombus of stars
pat-rhombus.statement: Print a rhombus of `*`. Each row has (N-i) leading spaces followed by N stars.
hints: Two inner loops per row: (n-i) spaces, then n stars.

approaches.name: Spaces then stars

pat-hollow-rect.title: Pattern 21: Hollow Rectangle
pat-hollow-rect.statement: Print an N x M hollow rectangle of `*` — border stars, interior spaces.
pat-hollow-rect.inputFormat: Two integers N M (rows and cols).
hints: Print `*` on first/last row, or first/last column; else print space.
Be careful with n==1 or m==1 edge cases — there's no interior.

approaches.name: Border check

pat-half-diamond.title: Pattern 10: Half Diamond
pat-half-diamond.statement: A half diamond: row i has i stars for i=1..N, then (2N-i) stars for i=N+1..2N-1. Each row ends the moment its star count reaches 0.
hints: First N rows increasing (i stars). Then N-1 rows decreasing.

approaches.name: Two loops
approaches.code:
for (int i = 1; i <= n; i++) { for (int j = 0; j < i; j++) System.out.print("*"); System.out.println(); }
for (int i = n - 1; i >= 1; i--) { for (int j = 0; j < i; j++) System.out.print("*"); System.out.println(); }

pat-diamond.title: Pattern 9: Star Diamond
pat-diamond.statement: Print a full diamond. Top half is a pyramid (N rows, row i has (N-i) spaces + (2i-1) stars). Bottom half is inverted pyramid (N rows, row i has (i-1) spaces + (2N-2i+1) stars).
hints: Top: spaces=n-i, stars=2i-1. Bottom: spaces=i-1, stars=2*(n-i)+1 for i=1..n.

approaches.name: Two halves

pat-butterfly.title: Pattern 20: Star Butterfly
pat-butterfly.statement: Print a butterfly pattern of stars. Top N rows: i stars, then 2*(N-i) spaces, then i stars. Bottom N rows are the mirror.
hints: Row i (top half): i stars, 2*(n-i) spaces, i stars. Bottom half mirrors it.

approaches.name: Symmetric halves

pat-num-pyramid.title: Pattern 11: Binary Triangle (0/1 alternating)
pat-num-pyramid.statement: Row i (1-indexed) prints (i) numbers: if the row starts with 1 (odd row number), alternate 1 0 1 0 ... Otherwise start with 0: 0 1 0 1 ... Each row has i numbers concatenated (no spaces).
hints: Track a `start` digit (1 if i is odd, 0 if even). Alternate by flipping with ^1 each column.

approaches.name: Alternate with XOR

pat-01-alternating.title: Pattern 12: Number Crown (mirrored triangle)
pat-01-alternating.statement: Row i prints: numbers 1..i, then (2*(n-i)) spaces, then numbers i..1. The result is N rows forming a mirrored number pyramid.
hints: Three parts per row: ascending 1..i, middle spaces, descending i..1.
Middle gap = 2*(n-i).

approaches.name: Three-part row

pat-num-crown.title: Pattern 13: Continuous Numbers Triangle
pat-num-crown.statement: Row i prints numbers i through i+(i-1) — i.e., i consecutive integers starting from i. No separators.
hints: Maintain a running counter that doesn't reset between rows.

approaches.name: Running counter

print-divisors.title: Print All Divisors
print-divisors.statement: Given N, print all positive divisors in ascending order, space-separated.
hints: Brute: loop i from 1 to N, check n % i == 0.
Optimal: loop i up to sqrt(N). If i divides n, then both i and n/i are divisors.
Collect into lower[] and upper[], then print lower ascending and upper descending.

approaches.name: Brute O(N)
Sqrt(N) optimal
approaches.explanation: Every divisor i <= sqrt(N) pairs with n/i. Collect both then sort.

prime-check.title: Check for Prime
prime-check.statement: Given N >= 1, print 'true' if prime, else 'false'. (1 is NOT prime.)
hints: If N < 2, not prime.
Check divisibility only up to sqrt(N) — if a*b=N, one of them is <= sqrt(N).
Skip even i > 2 for a small speedup.

approaches.name: sqrt(N) trial division

rec-print-name-n.title: Print a name N times (recursion)
rec-print-name-n.statement: Given integer N and a string S, print S N times on separate lines using recursion.
rec-print-name-n.inputFormat: Line 1: N. Line 2: string S (no spaces).
hints: Base: i > n → return.
Recursive: println(s); printN(i+1, n, s).

approaches.name: Tail recursion

rec-print-n-to-1.title: Print N to 1 using Recursion
rec-print-n-to-1.statement: Using recursion, print N, N-1, ..., 1 on one line space-separated.
hints: Base: i < 1 → return.
Print i, then recurse on i-1. Or recurse first then print (gives 1..N).

approaches.name: Print-then-recurse

hash-freq-array.title: Counting Frequencies of Array Elements
hash-freq-array.statement: Given an array, count each DISTINCT element's frequency. Print each unique element and its count on its own line, in the order of FIRST occurrence. Format each line as `value count`.
hash-freq-array.outputFormat: Each distinct value and its count on its own line, in first-occurrence order.
hints: Use a LinkedHashMap<Integer,Integer> to preserve insertion order.
For each element, freq.merge(v, 1, Integer::sum).
Iterate the map and print each entry on its own line.

approaches.name: LinkedHashMap

hash-highest-lowest-freq.title: Highest & Lowest Frequency Element
hash-highest-lowest-freq.statement: Given an array, print the element with HIGHEST frequency and the element with LOWEST frequency, on two separate lines. If there are ties, print the smallest value among the tied elements.
hash-highest-lowest-freq.outputFormat: Line 1: highest-frequency element. Line 2: lowest-frequency element.
hints: Build a frequency map.
Scan entries tracking max-count and min-count; tie-break by smallest key.

approaches.name: HashMap + single scan

sort-rec-bubble.title: Recursive Bubble Sort
sort-rec-bubble.statement: Implement bubble sort using recursion (no loops for the outer passes). Print the sorted array.
hints: Think of recursion as replacing the OUTER loop.
Each recursive call places the largest among a[0..n-1] at a[n-1], then recurses with n-1.

approaches.name: Recursive outer, iterative inner

sort-rec-insertion.title: Recursive Insertion Sort
sort-rec-insertion.statement: Implement insertion sort using recursion. Print the sorted array.
hints: The recursive structure just replaces the outer loop of insertion sort.
sort(a, i, n): if i == n return; insert(a, i); sort(a, i+1, n).
insert does the 'shift left' operation — can be iterative inside.

approaches.name: Outer recursion + iterative insert

sort-quick.title: Quick Sort
sort-quick.statement: Implement Quick Sort. Print the sorted array. (Use last-element-as-pivot partitioning.)
hints: Partition: pick pivot, rearrange so that everything < pivot is to its left, everything > pivot is to its right. Return pivot's final index.
Recurse on left and right halves separately.
On already-sorted input with last-element pivot, QS degrades to O(N²). Randomized pivot fixes it.

approaches.name: Lomuto partition (pivot = a[hi])
approaches.time: O(N log N) avg, O(N²) worst

pat-4-repnum.title: Pattern 4: Repeating Numbers (1, 22, 333)
pat-4-repnum.statement: Row i (1-indexed) prints the digit i, i times, with no separators.
hints: Outer loop i goes 1..N. Inner loop runs i times.
Inside the inner loop, print `i` (the row number) — not the column counter.

approaches.name: Print row index i times

pat-6-invnum.title: Pattern 6: Inverted Number Triangle (12345, 1234)
pat-6-invnum.statement: Row i prints the numbers 1 through (N-i+1), no separators. Row count goes from N down to 1.
hints: Outer loop i from 1 to N. Inner loop j from 1 to (N - i + 1).
Print j inside the inner loop.

approaches.name: Decreasing inner range

pat-8-invpyramid.title: Pattern 8: Inverted Star Pyramid (centered)
pat-8-invpyramid.statement: Top row is the widest, with (2N - 1) stars. Each subsequent row removes one star from each side. Row i has (i-1) leading spaces and (2*(N-i)+1) stars.
hints: Three sub-loops per row: leading spaces, stars, newline.
Row i: leading spaces = i - 1, stars = 2*(N - i) + 1.

approaches.name: Spaces shrink up, stars shrink down

pat-14-letter-tri.title: Pattern 14: Letter Triangle (A, AB, ABC)
pat-14-letter-tri.statement: Row i prints the letters A through ('A' + i - 1) with no separators.
hints: Loop j from 0 to i-1, print the char (char)('A' + j).
char arithmetic: 'A' + 1 = 'B', 'A' + 2 = 'C', etc.

approaches.name: Char arithmetic

pat-15-inv-letter-tri.title: Pattern 15: Inverted Letter Triangle (ABCDE, ABCD)
pat-15-inv-letter-tri.statement: Row i prints the letters A through ('A' + N - i). Letters per row decreases.
hints: Inner loop runs (N - i + 1) times.
Print (char)('A' + j) where j = 0..(N-i).

approaches.name: Decreasing inner letters

pat-16-rep-letter.title: Pattern 16: Repeating Letter Triangle (A, BB, CCC)
pat-16-rep-letter.statement: Row i prints the letter ('A' + i - 1) repeated i times.
hints: Compute the row's letter once: ch = (char)('A' + i - 1).
Inner loop runs i times, printing ch each time.

approaches.name: Per-row constant char

pat-17-sym-letter.title: Pattern 17: Symmetric Letter Pyramid (ABCBA)
pat-17-sym-letter.statement: Row i (1-indexed) has (N - i) leading spaces, then (2i - 1) letters: starts at 'A', climbs to ('A' + i - 1) at the middle, then descends back to 'A'.
hints: Three loops per row: leading spaces (N - i), letters going up to peak, letters coming down.
Peak letter at row i is ('A' + i - 1). Increment then decrement.
Total letters per row = 2i - 1 (odd).

approaches.name: Up-then-down character
approaches.explanation: Walk a counter ch from 'A' upward, then back down at the middle.

pat-18-rev-letter.title: Pattern 18: Reverse Letter Triangle (E, DE, CDE)
pat-18-rev-letter.statement: Row i prints letters from ('A' + N - i) up to ('A' + N - 1). The starting letter shifts left each row, ending at the same final letter.
hints: Row i has i letters. The first letter is at offset (N - i), the last is at (N - 1).
Loop j from (N - i) to (N - 1); print (char)('A' + j).

approaches.name: Sliding start position

pat-19-sym-stars.title: Pattern 19: Symmetric Stars (top wide, narrow middle, mirror)
pat-19-sym-stars.statement: Top half: row i (1..N) prints (N - i + 1) stars, then 2*(i - 1) spaces, then (N - i + 1) stars. Bottom half mirrors the top. Total rows: 2N. Width per row: 2N.
hints: Top half: row i prints (N - i + 1) stars, then 2*(i - 1) spaces, then (N - i + 1) stars.
Total width per row stays constant at 2N.
Bottom half is the same loop but with i going from N down to 1.

approaches.name: Two halves, mirror each other

pat-22-concentric.title: Pattern 22: Concentric Numbers (symmetric square)
pat-22-concentric.statement: Print a (2N-1) by (2N-1) grid of numbers. Each cell's value is N minus its minimum distance to any edge of the grid. The result is concentric rings: outer ring all N, next ring N-1, ..., center is 1. Values within a row are space-separated.
hints: Grid size: width = 2N - 1.
For cell (i, j) (0-indexed), distance from edges = min(i, j, width-1-i, width-1-j).
Cell value = N - distance. Outer ring has dist 0 = value N. Center has max dist = N - 1, value 1.
Print values space-separated; no trailing space at end of row.

approaches.name: Distance-from-edge formula
approaches.explanation: Each cell is N minus its min distance to any side of the grid. That formula creates concentric rings of equal value.

pat-square.title: Pattern 1: N x N Square of stars
pat-square.statement: Read an integer N. Print an N x N grid of `*` characters (each row on its own line).
pat-square.outputFormat: N lines, each with N asterisks.
hints: Use two nested loops — outer for rows, inner for columns.
Inside the inner loop, print `*` (no newline). After the inner loop finishes, print a newline.
System.out.print("*") vs System.out.println("*") — println adds a newline.

approaches.name: Nested loops
approaches.explanation: Two nested loops. Outer controls rows, inner prints one row of stars.

pat-rtri.title: Pattern 2: Right Half Pyramid (stars)
pat-rtri.statement: Print a right-angled triangle of `*`. Row i (1-indexed) has i stars.
pat-rtri.outputFormat: N lines; row i has i stars.
hints: Inner loop runs from 1 to i (or j <= i).
The key insight: stars per row = current row index.

approaches.name: Nested loops

pat-numtri.title: Pattern 3: Number Triangle
pat-numtri.statement: Row i prints numbers 1 2 ... i (concatenated with no separators).
hints: Replace `*` with `j` in the previous pattern.

approaches.name: Direct

pat-invrtri.title: Pattern 5: Inverted Right Half Pyramid
hints: Stars per row = n - i + 1, or loop j from i to n.

approaches.name: Direct

pat-pyramid.title: Pattern 7: Star Pyramid (centered)
pat-pyramid.statement: Print a centered star pyramid: row i has (n-i) leading spaces and (2i-1) stars.
pat-pyramid.outputFormat: N rows, increasing stars centered via leading spaces.
hints: Think of each row as three parts: leading spaces, stars, (optional trailing spaces).
Row i: spaces = n - i, stars = 2i - 1.

approaches.name: Three sub-loops

count-digits.title: Count Digits
count-digits.statement: Given a positive integer N, return the count of its digits.
hints: How many times can you divide N by 10 before it becomes 0?
while (n > 0) { n /= 10; count++; }
Or use (int) Math.log10(n) + 1 for O(1).

approaches.name: Iterative (divide by 10)
Logarithmic
approaches.code: System.out.println((int) Math.log10(n) + 1);

reverse-number.title: Reverse a Number
reverse-number.statement: Given N, print N with its digits reversed. Drop leading zeros in the reversed form (e.g., 120 -> 21).
hints: Build the reversed number: rev = rev * 10 + (n % 10), then n /= 10.
Because we print an int, leading zeros of the reversed form disappear naturally.

approaches.name: Digit-by-digit reversal
approaches.explanation: Pull last digit via %10, append to rev, divide n by 10.

check-palindrome.title: Palindrome Number
check-palindrome.statement: Return whether N equals its reverse. Print 'true' or 'false'.
hints: Reverse the number and compare to the original.
Save the original in a variable before reversing — because reversing mutates it.

approaches.name: Reverse and compare

gcd.title: GCD of Two Numbers
gcd.statement: Given two positive integers A and B, print their greatest common divisor.
gcd.inputFormat: Two space-separated integers A and B.
hints: Euclidean algorithm: gcd(a, b) = gcd(b, a % b) until b == 0.
When b becomes 0, the answer is a.

approaches.name: Brute force
approaches.explanation: Try every divisor from min(a,b) down to 1.

approaches.name: Euclidean (optimal)
approaches.explanation: gcd(a,b) = gcd(b, a mod b). Terminates when b == 0.
approaches.code:
while (b != 0) { int t = b; b = a % b; a = t; }
System.out.println(a);

armstrong.title: Armstrong Number
armstrong.statement: An Armstrong (narcissistic) 3-digit number equals the sum of the cubes of its digits (e.g., 153 = 1³+5³+3³). Given a 3-digit N, print 'true' if it is Armstrong, else 'false'.
hints: Extract each digit via % 10 then / 10.
Sum the cubes; compare to the original.

approaches.name: Digit extraction + sum of cubes

rec-print-n.title: Print 1 to N using Recursion
rec-print-n.statement: Using recursion (no loops), print integers 1 to N space-separated on one line.
hints: Base case: if i > n, return.
Recursive case: append i (and a space) to `out`, then call print(i+1, n).

approaches.name: Tail-style recursion

rec-sum-n.title: Sum of first N (recursive)
hints: Base: sum(0) = 0.
Recursive: sum(n) = n + sum(n-1).

approaches.name: Functional recursion

rec-factorial.title: Factorial (recursive)
rec-factorial.statement: Compute N! recursively. Assume N <= 20 (fits in long).
hints: Base: fact(0) = fact(1) = 1.
Recursive: fact(n) = n * fact(n-1).

approaches.name: Recursion

rec-reverse-array.title: Reverse Array (recursive)
rec-reverse-array.statement: Reverse an array in-place using recursion. Print the result space-separated.
rec-reverse-array.inputFormat: Line 1: N. Line 2: N integers space-separated.
hints: Swap a[l] with a[r], then recurse on (l+1, r-1).
Base: l >= r (nothing to swap).

approaches.name: Two-pointer recursion

rec-palindrome-str.title: Palindrome String (recursive)
rec-palindrome-str.statement: Check whether a lowercase string is a palindrome using recursion. Print 'true' or 'false'.
rec-palindrome-str.inputFormat: A single string of lowercase letters.
hints: If l >= r, return true.
If s.charAt(l) != s.charAt(r), return false.
Else recurse (l+1, r-1).

approaches.name: Two-pointer recursion

rec-fibonacci.title: Fibonacci (recursive)
rec-fibonacci.statement: Compute the Nth Fibonacci number. F(0)=0, F(1)=1, F(n)=F(n-1)+F(n-2). Use recursion.
hints: Base cases: fib(0)=0, fib(1)=1.
Recursive: fib(n) = fib(n-1) + fib(n-2).
Note: plain recursion is O(2^N). DP/memoization makes it O(N).

approaches.name: Plain recursion
Memoized (DP)
approaches.explanation: Cache results; each fib(k) computed once.

sort-selection.title: Selection Sort
sort-selection.statement: Implement selection sort on the given array. Print the sorted array ascending, space-separated.
hints: For each position i, find the minimum element in a[i..n-1] and swap it into a[i].
Two loops: outer from 0 to n-1, inner searches for min.

approaches.name: Selection sort
approaches.explanation: Find the min of the unsorted suffix, swap to the front.

sort-bubble.title: Bubble Sort
sort-bubble.statement: Implement bubble sort. Print the sorted array.
hints: In each outer pass, compare adjacent pairs and swap if out of order.
After pass i, the largest i+1 elements are at the end. Inner loop can stop at n-i-1.
Early exit: if no swaps in a pass, array is sorted.

approaches.name: Bubble sort with early exit

sort-insertion.title: Insertion Sort
sort-insertion.statement: Implement insertion sort. Print the sorted array.
hints: Think of your left hand of cards: pick the next card and slide it left until it's in the right place.
For each i from 1 to n-1: let key = a[i]; shift a[0..i-1] right while they are > key; place key.

approaches.name: Insertion sort

sort-merge.title: Merge Sort
sort-merge.statement: Implement merge sort. Print the sorted array.
hints: Divide: split into halves; recursively sort each half.
Conquer (merge): with two sorted halves, merge into one sorted array using two pointers.
Base case: l >= r (single element or empty).

approaches.name: Divide and conquer
approaches.explanation: log N levels of division, each level does O(N) merging.

arr-largest.title: Largest element in array
arr-largest.statement: Find the largest element in an array.
hints: Track the running maximum as you scan.
Initialize max to a[0] (or Integer.MIN_VALUE), then update on each element.

approaches.name: Linear scan

arr-second-largest.title: Second Largest (without sort)
arr-second-largest.statement: Find the second largest DISTINCT element. If it doesn't exist (e.g., all equal), print -1.
hints: Track both largest and secondLargest as you scan in O(N).
When you find a new largest L, the old largest becomes second.
Skip values equal to the current largest when updating second.

approaches.name: Sort (easy but O(N log N))
Single-pass O(N)
approaches.explanation: Maintain largest and second. When a new largest appears, demote the old largest.

arr-is-sorted.title: Check if Array is Sorted (non-decreasing)
arr-is-sorted.statement: Return 'true' if the array is sorted non-decreasingly; else 'false'.
hints: For every i from 1 to n-1, check a[i-1] <= a[i]. If any pair violates, return false.

approaches.name: Single scan

arr-remove-duplicates.title: Remove Duplicates from Sorted Array
arr-remove-duplicates.statement: Given a SORTED array, remove duplicates in-place and print: (line 1) K = count of distinct; (line 2) the first K elements of the modified array.
arr-remove-duplicates.inputFormat: Line 1: N. Line 2: N sorted integers.
arr-remove-duplicates.outputFormat: Line 1: K. Line 2: the K distinct values (ascending).
hints: Two pointers: `i` (write index) and `j` (scan index).
Start with i=0; for each j from 1 to n-1, if a[j] != a[i], advance i and write a[j] to a[i].

approaches.name: Two pointers (write + scan)

arr-rotate-1.title: Left Rotate Array by 1
arr-rotate-1.statement: Rotate the array left by one position (first element becomes last). Print the result.
hints: Save a[0] to a temp. Shift each element one to the left. Put temp at a[n-1].

approaches.name: Shift in place

arr-rotate-d.title: Left Rotate Array by D
arr-rotate-d.statement: Left rotate the array by D positions. Print the result.
arr-rotate-d.inputFormat: Line 1: N D. Line 2: N integers.
hints: Normalize d: d = d % n.
Reverse trick: reverse a[0..d-1], reverse a[d..n-1], reverse the whole array.
Or: use a temp array of size d for the first D elements.

approaches.name: Reverse trick (optimal)
Using temp array
approaches.explanation: Save first D to temp; shift the rest; place temp at end.

arr-move-zeros.title: Move Zeros to End
arr-move-zeros.statement: Move all 0s to the end while preserving the relative order of non-zeros. Print the modified array.
hints: Two pointers: `j` points at the first zero; scan with `i`.
When a[i] != 0 and i > j, swap a[i] with a[j], then advance j.
If there are no zeros initially, j stays at -1 (or n) — handle that by scanning for the first zero first.

approaches.name: Two pointers (single pass)

arr-union-sorted.title: Union of Two Sorted Arrays
arr-union-sorted.statement: Given two sorted arrays, print their union (distinct elements in ascending order).
arr-union-sorted.inputFormat: Line 1: N M. Line 2: N sorted ints. Line 3: M sorted ints.
hints: Two pointers i, j over a, b. At each step take the smaller; if equal, take one and advance both.
Track lastAdded to skip consecutive duplicates.

approaches.name: Two pointers merge

arr-missing-num.title: Find Missing Number (1..N)
arr-missing-num.statement: You are given an array of N-1 distinct integers, each in range 1..N. Find the single missing number.
hints: Sum approach: expected = N*(N+1)/2, subtract actual sum.
XOR approach: XOR of 1..N XOR all array elements = missing (handles overflow).

approaches.name: Sum formula
XOR (overflow-safe)
approaches.explanation: x ^ x = 0, x ^ 0 = x. XOR of full range cancels out present values.

arr-max-ones.title: Max Consecutive 1s
arr-max-ones.statement: In a binary (0/1) array, return the maximum length of a consecutive run of 1s.
arr-max-ones.inputFormat: Line 1: N. Line 2: N values 0 or 1.
hints: Walk once. Track current run. On 0, reset. Track best.

approaches.name: Running count

arr-single-number.title: Single Number (others twice)
arr-single-number.statement: Every element appears exactly twice except one which appears once. Find it in O(N) time and O(1) space.
hints: XOR is magic here: x ^ x = 0 and x ^ 0 = x.
XOR all elements — pairs cancel; the lonely one remains.

approaches.name: XOR fold
